title: comic_brief — the upstream hand-off (idea → Step-0 → comic.json)
description: The SEMANTIC brief a USER's agent emits from a fuzzy idea. It decides WHAT the comic depicts; Step-0 compiles it into a schema-valid comic.json (schemas/comic.schema.json); run_comic.py bakes + verifies. Mirrors method_figure_brief.schema.json. See references/comic_authoring.md.

logline.description: one-sentence premise the whole comic must land

thesis.description: the point a reader should feel by the end

cast.description: the recurring characters + their locked identity

identity_ref.description: project-relative .png canonical sheet (BYO; null → project canonical)

traits.description: locked visual traits, e.g. 'blue hoodie','no beard'

worlds.description: the palette/world tags this comic uses (define them in ART_BIBLE.md), e.g. warm-lab, dark-cyber

beats.description: the story beats, in reading order (one per page)

bake_lang.description: which dialogue language gets baked into the image

world.description: which world tag (must be one of brief.worlds)

scene.description: what's in the panel — props, staging, composition (becomes condition.scene)

identity_ref.description: BYO character sheet for this panel; null → project canonical

figure.description: the technical figure this panel carries (if any)

blueprint_svg.description: project-relative .svg YOU author = the content authority (→ condition.content_svg)

headline_literals.description: the exact numbers/keys/code tokens that MUST survive the bake (→ condition.expected_literals; REQUIRED if blueprint_svg is set)

text_mode.description: baked = dialogue drawn into the image; html = HTML bubble overlay in the viewer
